title: Amsterdam Boat Rental Guide: Costs, Tips & Yacht Charter 2026
html:
Navigating Amsterdam’s canals and the adjacent IJsselmeer by private boat offers an unforgettable perspective on the city. Departing from marinas such as Sixhaven or Amsterdam Marina, a half-day motorboat hire typically ranges from EUR 250 to EUR 600, while crewed luxury yacht charters start around EUR 1,500 per day. BoatHire24 connects you with verified listings across these price bands.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Amsterdam?

Daily charter rates for a private motorboat in Amsterdam range from EUR 250 to EUR 3,500 depending on the vessel and season. For a full breakdown of prices and availability, a yacht charter Amsterdam search on BoatHire24 will show you real-time options from local operators. A shared canal tour can cost as little as EUR 20 per person, but for a private experience expect to pay more.

Where to set off in Amsterdam

Amsterdam’s network of canals and its access to the IJsselmeer offer several excellent departure points. Sixhaven Marina lies just north of Central Station, providing quick access to both city canals and open water. Amsterdam Marina, on the IJburg islands, is ideal for larger yachts heading towards Markermeer. Other trusted launch points include Jachthaven IJburg, Jachthaven Durgerdam, and Port of Amsterdam for sea-bound trips. Each offers parking, fuel, and facilities for charter guests.

Which boat to rent

  1. Sloop or electric boat, perfect for couples exploring the inner canals; capacity 4, 6 guests; from EUR 250 for three hours.
  2. Small motorboat (4, 6 m), ideal for a family day trip on the Amstel River; capacity up to 8; from EUR 400 for half a day.
  3. Catamaran or sailing yacht, best for groups wanting to sail the IJsselmeer; capacity 10, 12; from EUR 900 for a full day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (Maiora, Azimut, Sunseeker), suitable for corporate events or special celebrations; capacity up to 14 with crew; from EUR 1,800 per day. See the Maiora 26m listing for an example of a crewed vessel available in Amsterdam.

Costs for boat rental in Amsterdam

  • Shared canal tour: EUR 20, 50 per person for one hour.
  • Small motorboat (half day, no skipper): EUR 250, 600.
  • Catamaran (full day, skipper included): EUR 900, 1,500.
  • Crewed luxury yacht (full day, catering optional): EUR 1,500, 3,500.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Amsterdam?

For most smaller motorboats (under 15 hp or limited length) you can rent without a licence, but larger vessels and yachts may require an ICC or equivalent. The operator will confirm exact requirements when you book, and many offer skippered charters so you can relax without a licence. A valid ID and a damage deposit are standard.

For a quiet afternoon, head east from Amsterdam Marina to the winding channels of the Waterland region; the wind tends to be light in June, making for easy cruising.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 20 shared tour, EUR 3,500 crewed yacht per day
  • Peak months: May, June, July, August, September
  • Typical trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Maximum guest capacity: from 4 to 14+ depending on boat
  • Currency: EUR (all transactions in euros)

Questions about boat rental in Amsterdam

Can I rent a boat in Amsterdam without a licence?

Yes, many small electric and petrol boats can be rented without a licence, though operators may require a brief safety briefing.

Is it possible to rent a yacht with a skipper?

Yes, crewed charters are widely available, and this is the easiest way to explore the IJsselmeer or Amsterdam’s canals without navigational experience.

How far can I travel from Amsterdam in a rented boat?

From the city centre, the distance to the historic town of Muiden is approximately 15 nautical miles, a comfortable two-hour cruise one way.

What is the best time of year for a yacht charter in Amsterdam?

The period from late April to early October offers the best weather, with July and August as the warmest months.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Amsterdam and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
